heading: Duties of consular officers
label: 10704 Duties of consular officers
text: When a seaman dies outside the United States leaving money or property not on board a vessel, the consular officer nearest the place at which the money and property is located shall claim and take charge of it.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource section (U.S. Code)1070446:624Section 10704 requires consular officers to take charge of the property (not left on board a vessel) of deceased seamen who have died outside the United States.
